steps.title: Recognize the discriminant condition and rearrange
steps.content:
When a quadratic equation has exactly one real solution, the discriminant must equal zero. Rearrange $4x^2 + 21 = 48x + r$ into standard form by moving all terms to the left:
$$4x^2 - 48x + (21 - r) = 0$$
This gives us $a = 4$, $b = -48$, and $c = 21 - r$.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant formula and solve for r
steps.content:
Substitute the coefficients into the discriminant formula:
$$(-48)^2 - 4(4)(21 - r) = 0$$
$$2304 - 16(21 - r) = 0$$
$$2304 - 336 + 16r = 0$$
$$1968 + 16r = 0$$
$$r = -123$$
